title: Projects Config (v1)
description: Per-project overrides that give UX-testing agents and other audience-aware tooling domain context without hardcoding it into harness files. Lives at ~/.claude/local/projects.config.json (never committed). Keys under 'projects' are free-form path hints (absolute, home-relative, or a substring the harness can match against the working directory). Each entry describes the project type and a one-line example persona the agents can adopt.

projects.description: Map from project path hint to project metadata. The object may be empty. Keys are arbitrary strings — the harness performs a longest-prefix match against the current working directory.

type.description: High-level project shape. Drives which UX agents and tests are relevant (e.g. web projects get the end-user-tester agent, CLI projects skip it).

example_persona.description: One-line description of the target user, used by UX agents as domain context. Example: 'A small-business owner checking their dashboard on mobile.'

preferred_color_keywords.description: Optional list of palette keywords (e.g. 'blue', 'neutral', 'warm') that describe the project's visual language. Used by design-adjacent tooling to stay consistent.
